title: An Optimized Protocol for Packaging Pseudotyped Integrase Defective Lentivirus
author: Sengupta, Mukherjee, et al.
materials: cells may be contaminated by mycoplasma; (

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Prepare lentivector DNA and packaging plasmid mixAdd 1 ~ 1.6 ml plain DMEM (serum and antibiotic free) to an Eppendorf tube; add 4.5 μg lentivector and 45 μl packaging plasmid DNAinto the DMEM. Mix by pipetting for 10 times.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Prepare transfection mixAdd 55 μl PureFection into the same tube. Vertex for 10 s and incubate the DMEM-Plasmid-PureFectionmixture at room temperature for 15 min.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Apply transfection mix to producer cellsAdd DMEM-Plasmid-PureFectionmixture drop-wise to the dish, and swirl to disperse evenly; return the dish to the cell culture incubator at 37 °C with 5 % CO2; change the medium 12 ~ 24 h after transfection (optional).

steps.title: poor transfection efficiency due to too...
steps.instruction: poor transfection efficiency due to too high or too low density of producer cells. (

steps.title: Poor quality of lentivector or packaging plasmid DNA
steps.instruction: Poor quality of lentivector or packaging plasmid DNA. (

steps.title: Inadequate ratios of plasmid mix to the transfection reagent
steps.instruction: Inadequate ratios of plasmid mix to the transfection reagent. To avoid these, plate cells to achieve 70 ~ 80 % confluency at transfection stage and use endotoxin-free plasmid purification kit to prepare lentivector expressing the transgene and packaging plasmids.

steps.title: producer HEK293TN cells are of poor...
steps.instruction: producer HEK293TN cells are of poor quality due to over confluence; (

steps.title: of cells
steps.instruction: of cells. In those cases, one should revive a new batch of producer cells and maintain them in the logarithmic growth phase.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Other factors which could affect virus quality are: The packaging limit for the lentiviral system, which is ~8.5 kb from 5′ → 3′ LTR. The efficiency of packaging drops significantly when cDNA size increases. For a 3 kb insert, the titers and expression level can be 10-fold less compared to that ofa 1 kb insert. Repeated sequences can also cause problems in transgene expression as recently reported [17].

steps.title: Cell Culture and Transduction
steps.instruction: Human embryonic kidney HEK293-TN (LV900A-1, SBI, Mountain View, CA) and fibrosarcoma HT1080 (ATCC, Manassas, VA) cells were maintained in high glucose Dulbecco’s Minimal Essential Medium (DMEM) supplemented with 10 % FBS, 2 mMGlutaMax (Life Technologies), 100 U/ml penicillin and 100 U/ml streptomycin. Transduction reagent TransDux (LV850A-1, SBI, Mountain View, CA) was used to infect cells with virus to enhance the transducing efficiency. CD511B-1 (GFP reporter) and LV605A-1 (DualGFP-RFP reporter) from SBI were the constructs used to make WT and integrase defective lentiviral particles for most of the studies. These constructs were assembled by a combination of PCR and seamless fusion method as reported before [15, 16].

steps.title: Step-by-step Protocol (bench time 110 min, total time 6 days)
steps.instruction: Prepare lentivector DNA and packaging plasmid mixAdd 1 ~ 1.6 ml plain DMEM (serum and antibiotic free) to an Eppendorf tube; add 4.5 μg lentivector and 45 μl packaging plasmid DNAinto the DMEM. Mix by pipetting for 10 times.

steps.title: Step-by-step Protocol (bench time 110 min, total time 6 days)
steps.instruction: Prepare transfection mixAdd 55 μl PureFection into the same tube. Vertex for 10 s and incubate the DMEM-Plasmid-PureFectionmixture at room temperature for 15 min.

steps.title: Step-by-step Protocol (bench time 110 min, total time 6 days)
steps.instruction: Apply transfection mix to producer cellsAdd DMEM-Plasmid-PureFectionmixture drop-wise to the dish, and swirl to disperse evenly; return the dish to the cell culture incubator at 37 °C with 5 % CO2; change the medium 12 ~ 24 h after transfection (optional).
